data.title: Amazon Stock (AMZN) Slips despite Reports of Deeper AI Integration at Cloud Unit
data.description:
E-commerce giant Amazon's (AMZN) cloud unit is now building on its recently launched AI-powered tool for partner sales. According to The Information, the unit is developing an AI agent to automate tasks such as sales and business development — areas where it recently trimmed its workforce.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade AMZN with leverage
  • New trading tool for AMZN bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

The agent is also being tailored to support employee efforts to deliver rapid service to clients in areas that require technical expertise. The report comes as Amazon's efforts to integrate its newly launched Kiro AI into its business have raised concerns due to outages linked to the coding tool.

Amazon Deepens AI Integration at AWS

Across the world, enterprises are racing to integrate AI into their workflow for greater efficiency. Payments technology firm Block (XYZ) recently attracted attention when it slashed its workforce by 40%, noting that AI was making it possible for the firm to operate more efficiently with even smaller teams.

However, Amazon is also aiming to reap the benefits of such adoption. In mid-March, AWS dubbed its new AI-based tool AWS Partner Central agents. The tools were designed to help customers gain insight into sales pipelines, boost deal progression, and identify funding opportunities.

"AWS Partner Central agents automate administrative tasks and streamline opportunity management, guiding actions across co-selling, funding, and beyond," AWS noted in a blog post.

Amazon Faces AI Competition

However, beyond the opportunity for integration, Amazon's business is also bound to encounter more competition from the rise of AI shopping agents — bots that conduct purchase activities on behalf of their users.

This could lead to users shifting away from retail platforms such as Amazon toward AI assistants like ChatGPT.

What Is the AMZN Price Target?

On Wall Street, Amazon's shares continue to enjoy a Strong Buy consensus rating. This is based on 40 Buys and three Holds assigned by 43 analysts over the past three months.

In addition, the average AMZN price target of $282.39 implies about 36% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: "The Algorithm" Runs Down Tesla's Successes, Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Notches Up
data.description:
There is a new book coming out, reports note, that runs down some of the early successes of electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA). The book is called The Algorithm, and features former Tesla President Jon McNeill as he details how Tesla managed to innovate in a world where developing growth can be especially difficult. Investors were mildly pleased, based on the fractional bump in Tesla shares seen in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade TSLA with leverage
  • New trading tool for TSLA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

McNeill offered quite a bit of commentary in this book, as he was well-placed to do so. One point that McNeill brings up in the book is "question every requirement." This is designed to find "dumb or unnecessary" requirements embedded in the operation. If these can be removed, do so as a means to save time or money, often both.

Conducting detailed process analysis is also a vital point, and such process analysis should begin with a focus on the customer. Making a process more agile can be done simply by finding out what parts of the process the customer is not paying for and removing them. McNeill describes how, originally, the process to buy a Tesla required about 64 mouse clicks. Detailed process analysis followed, and the number dropped to about a dozen.

Tesla Sharers Gain

If you happen to share a Tesla with your wife, kids, or just down-on-his-luck brother, there is good news ahead for you. A new feature was recently added, quietly, to the Tesla mobile app. That feature is real-time visibility of the profile of the active driver. With this, owners can see who is behind the wheel currently, or who was last to drive the vehicle in question.

Users will need not only the latest Tesla app to use the tool, but also the 2026.8 vehicle software version. This offers top-notch personalization, including things like seat position and steering wheel height that change between drivers. It also allows for restricted profiles for younger drivers.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 32.18% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $399.25 per share implies 4.76% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Upbeat Easter Spending Projection Masks Slowing Business Activity
data.description:
The market received two seemingly conflicting data points on Tuesday. While consumer spending is estimated by the National Retail Federation to rise 5.5% to a record-high $24.9 billion this Easter, the S&P Global Flash Composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) fell to an 11-month low of 51.4 in March. Factors contributing to the decline include higher input prices driven by rising energy costs and business uncertainty from the U.S.-Iran war.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

However, the two data points reflect different parts of the economy and factor in different kinds of data.

Easter Sales to Push Higher Despite Broader Growth Slowdown

The record Easter spending projection reflects strong consumer demand in a specific retail segment during one point in time. This signals that Americans are willing to spend on seasonal goods, including decorations, sweets, and Easter eggs.

On the other hand, the PMI measures overall business activity across manufacturing and services, and its drop to an 11-month low indicates that broader economic growth is slowing. In other words, consumers may still spend in targeted areas, even as overall business activity weakens.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Finland Pauses Plan to Migrate Its Election System to Amazon Web Services amid Political Tensions
data.description:
Finland has decided to pause its plan to migrate its election system to Amazon Web Services (AMZN) due to growing concerns in Europe about relying on U.S. technology companies. Instead of moving to the cloud, the country will keep its election platform on servers located in Finland until after the April 2027 general election. This comes after the Justice Ministry reviewed an earlier decision from spring 2025 to switch to Amazon's cloud system.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade AMZN with leverage
  • New trading tool for AMZN bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

At the same time, the review was influenced by changes in the global political environment. More specifically, the Justice Ministry said that the international situation has shifted over the past year, which led it to rethink how and where such an important system should be hosted. Because of this uncertainty, Finland is choosing to delay the move and will decide on a long-term solution at a later time.

It's also important to understand that even though voting in Finland is done using paper ballots, the digital system still plays a major role. In fact, it manages key information such as polling locations, candidates, and voter lists, and is also used to count votes and publish the final results. As a result, where this system is stored and operated is very important, which is why the government is taking a more cautious approach.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 40 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $280 per share implies 34.5% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: "…Ran Like a Manufacturing Facility": Starbucks Stock (NASDAQ:SBUX) Slips as it Realizes Key Problem
data.description:
Coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) was once a great destination for people to go and spend some time in an unusual environment. But as we found recently, Starbucks forgot "the power of hospitality," instead focusing on getting coffee out the door in huge numbers. In fact, CEO Brian Niccol recently noted that Starbucks "…ran like a manufacturing facility" at one point. This revelation will likely help going forward, but it did little for share prices today. Starbucks shares were down over 1.5% in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Basically, reports note, Niccol got a look at Starbucks in the early days of his administration as CEO and noted that the atmosphere had taken a back seat to the process. It was also hurting the customer experience, as customers were left with long waits for products of varying quality. It certainly proved no help to the baristas, who were trying frantically to keep up with an environment that was almost industrial.

And that prompted Niccol's large-scale philosophy shift, as Starbucks tries to reassert itself as a "third-place" where people can go for purely social reasons. Niccol also believes that an improved overall experience will also bring customers back, a point that is not hard to believe, but may be strained in an environment of economic uncertainty.

The Hannah Montana Special

Remember Hannah Montana? The Disney (DIS) show from 20 years ago that featured a pre-twerking Miley Cyrus as a girl with a celebrity alter-ego, trying to live in two different worlds as best she could? Starbucks is taking advantage of this phenomenon with a new hidden menu item, the Hannah's Secret Popstar Refresher.

The drink involves strawberry acai and raspberry cold foam, with a little extra raspberry syrup. Long-time fans of the show might point out here that raspberry was a no-go for the character in the show due to a pie-eating contest that left her sick, but apparently "made peace with raspberries 20 years later," according to Starbucks. This comes at the same time as the upcoming Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special airing March 24.

Is Starbucks Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SBUX stock based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 2.14% loss in its share price over the past year, the average SBUX price target of $101 per share implies 9.62% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 3/24/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading lower on Tuesday after Iran denied holding direct peace talks with the U.S.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

On Monday, President Trump said he would hold off on attacking Iranian infrastructure for five days following "very good and productive conversations" between the two sides. He added that a peace deal could be reached within the same time period. Iran denied direct talks with the U.S., though it acknowledged indirect communication via intermediaries.

Iran 'Highly Unlikely' to Concede on Key U.S. Conditions

Iran has issued two key demands to end the war, which are reparations for damages and a guarantee against future military attacks. On the other hand, the U.S. seeks an end to all Iranian uranium enrichment, the elimination of existing stockpiles, and a halt to ballistic missile development. Iran is "highly unlikely" to agree to these terms, three senior Israeli officials told Reuters.

Rising Brent crude futures (BZ) are also pressuring stocks and are up by nearly 4% on the day, just below the $100 level.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Comcast Stock (NASDAQ:CMCSA) Rises, Keeps Focus on Streaming and Wireless Growth
data.description:
Entertainment giant Comcast (CMCSA) is a company in flux right now. With one of its primary revenue streams—linear cable—starting to fade and two of its newest revenue streams still in growth patterns, it is clear the Comcast of today is much different from the Comcast of five years ago. And investors are getting behind the company as it focuses on its newer growth streams, giving Comcast shares a nearly 2% boost in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Recently, Comcast hit the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media and Telecom Conference to talk about its growth plans. Its plan of action was pretty clear: take what it can get from the slumping sides, and put more focus on the growing sides. This will not be easy, though, as many other companies smell growth opportunities in streaming and wireless alike. Thus, Comcast has no shortage of competition in these fields. That will also likely be the case for the foreseeable future.

Peacock is proving to be a winner. Performance was up about $700 million in a year-over-year comparison, and Comcast Business is still pulling in substantial revenue at around $15 billion. The results speak for themselves, especially considering Comcast has staged $50 billion in share buybacks. That plus other returns to shareholders add up to a cumulative total of around $70 billion.

Peacock Spreading its Tail Feathers

Peacock, meanwhile, is in full display mode, bringing more and more content to the platform. One of the biggest moves Peacock has made is bringing in one of the few Marvel properties not covered by Disney (DIS), the Blade trilogy. That might eventually get taken back to Paramount+, as Warner (WBD) owns the rights, but for now, Peacock has the trilogy to itself.

But Peacock also lost something recently, as WWE NXT has been pulled from the platform. Everything that was on Peacock has been shifted to the WWE NXT YouTube channel, reports note. The issue is mostly one of rights, as wrestling content recently underwent a series of shifts in that vein.

Is Comcast Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on CMCSA stock based on five Buys, 10 Holds and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 21.47% loss in its share price over the past year, the average CMCSA price target of $32.45 per share implies 9.81% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Reverses Lossses as Iran Signals 'Outreach' with U.S.; Gulf States Weigh Military Options
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading slightly higher on Tuesday and has reversed its morning losses amid new developments in the U.S.-Iran war.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

An Iranian official told CNN that the country has received "outreach" from the U.S. and is willing to discuss "sustainable" proposals to end the war. "There has been outreach between the United States and Iran, initiated by Washington, in recent days, but nothing that has reached the level of full-on negotiations," said the source, adding that Iran has received messages from "various intermediaries."

Iran's two main demands to end the war are reparations for damages and a binding guarantee against future military attacks. On the other side, the U.S. seeks an end to all Iranian uranium enrichment, the elimination of existing stockpiles, and a halt to ballistic missile development. Iran is "highly unlikely" to agree to these terms, three senior Israeli officials told Reuters.

Meanwhile, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates have warned that they would join the war against Iran if the country launches attacks on key power and water infrastructure sites. Iran has targeted both Gulf states, along with Bahrain and Kuwait, with drone and missile strikes over the past 24 hours.

Elsewhere, the effects of the war are beginning to materialize in economic data. The S&P Global Flash Composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) fell to 51.4 in March, registering an 11-month low. A reading above 50 indicates economic expansion, while a reading below 50 indicates contraction.

"Companies are reporting a hit to demand from the additional uncertainty and cost of living impact generated by the conflict," said Chris Williamson, Chief Business Economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.27% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan
